body: OpenCode's `opencode run --session <id>` spawns a fresh process per turn but reuses the SQLite session row. The daemon observes one continuous ses_… id across the 20s idle window even though the underlying process exits and is re-launched. Recording validates 4/4 phases (session birth, turn1 cycle, session re-emergence after idle); the second turn's working-state transition isn't always captured because opencode's per-turn process can complete inside a single classifier tick.
caveats: No persistent PID across turns: irrlicht's PID-liveness check doesn't apply; session continuity comes from the SQLite session table.
Second turn may complete too quickly to observe a ready→working transition.
